Description: Enables commands to be entered into the server console. WARNING: May cause crashes.
Enables output messages to the console from tasks and world building commands.
URL displayed on the Message of the Day button. Example: https://google.com
Text displayed next to the MOTD website button. If empty, displays 'Website'.
Minutes between auto-saves (decimal, 10.0 - 240.0).
If enabled, saves the game when a character logs out. Helpful for single player.
Runs the self-cleaning and self-sustaining routines (hourly/3h/24h) when the server starts. Recommended for servers that are not 24x7.
Number of days a character must exist before a player can delete them (decimal).
If enabled, players can create an account by just typing a name and password.
The port the server listens on.
IP address for external connections. Leave blank for auto-detect.
The name of your server/world.
If enabled, your public IP address will be auto-detected for external connections.
Blackjack tables retain their settings/info across server restarts. Better for persistent worlds.
Percent chance a floor trap will trigger (minimum 5).
If enabled, characters are notified when they make a saving throw to avoid a trap.
If enabled, dungeon environments will have random ambient sounds.
If enabled, strange portals leading to deep dungeons will have an exit portal.
Gold cost to bribe the Assassin Guildmaster to remove a murder count. Set below 1000 to disable. Assassin guild members pay half.
If enabled, players can use Magic Scissors/Hammer to change classic artifact appearance (name is always retained).
Minutes before a player corpse turns to bones.
Minutes before player bones decay. Combined with Corpse Decay is total time to recover belongings.
If enabled, characters cannot use macros to improve skills quickly.
Rate of stat gain (decimal). 50.0 = slow, 10.0 = fast.
Minutes between stat gains (decimal, 5.0 - 60.0).
If enabled, new characters can choose the alien origin route: grandmaster 40 skills but start with no gold/skills and suffer penalties.
0 = disabled. 1 = human-sized creatures. 2 = includes larger creatures. 3 = includes giants/balrons.
If enabled, characters don't get hungry/thirsty inside banks, inns, or houses.
Minutes between hunger and thirst checks (5-60).
Base gold cost to join a guild (minimum 200). Increases with each guild joined if Guild Fee Increase is enabled.
If enabled, guild join fee increases based on number of prior guild memberships.
Maximum value for player resistances (physical, cold, fire, poison, energy). Range: 40-90.
Character level at which resurrection tribute fees begin (1-100).
Gold per character level for resurrection cost (minimum 1).
Percentage of spell damage dealt to monsters (25-200).
Percentage of spell damage dealt to player characters (25-200).
Maximum lower reagent percentage from equipment (0-100). 0 disables the attribute.
Lower reagent percentage per enchantment level.
Maximum lower mana percentage from equipment (0-100). 0 disables the attribute.
Lower mana percentage per enchantment level.
Multiplier for HP/Mana/Stamina based on attributes (decimal, 0.5 - 3.0). A strength of 100 with 2.0 gives 200 HP.
If enabled, characters can set a custom title in the HELP section.
Minimum gold a new character starts with (max 10000).
Maximum gold a new character starts with (max 10000).
If enabled, powerscrolls must be consumed in order: 105 -> 110 -> 115 -> 120 -> 125.
Percent chance an item will be unidentified (minimum 10).
If enabled, items requiring identification can only be identified from within backpack.
Comma-separated skill levels for guaranteed ID saving throws. Each entry guarantees an item at that skill level. Default: 0,30,60
Determines how often treasure appears on corpses and chests (0-100). Higher = more loot.
Minutes before a player can take a new bulletin board quest.
Minutes before a player can take a new sage artifact quest. Default 20160 = 14 days.
Percent of gold from creatures, treasure, cargo, museum searches, shoppes, and some quests (5-100).
Percentage modifier for bulletin board and thief note quest gold rewards (0-250).
If enabled, characters can steal decoration artifacts multiple times. Otherwise only once each.
If enabled, characters get lucrative pedestal bag/box items only once every couple of days.
If enabled, characters receive a warning before entering quest-locked Skara Brae.
If enabled, characters receive a warning before entering quest-locked Bottle City.
Extra GM skill points (0-10). Each point adds 100 skill points. 0 = normal.
Minimum weapon/tactics skill for special abilities (decimal, minimum 20.0). Each ability requires 10 points above previous.
Faster skill gain (0-10). 0 = normal, 10 = fastest.
Maximum skill from training dummies, training daemons, and archery buttes (decimal).
Maximum Stealing skill from pickpocket dips (decimal).
Multiplies skill gain checks on training dummies, pickpocket dips, and archery buttes (1-100). Greater than 1 enables macroing.
If enabled, craft 1, 10, or 100 items at a time. Also enables macroing and disables all-resource crafting (arrows, shafts, kindling).
If enabled (requires Craft Many), shows 1/10/100 buttons next to each crafting line item.
Multiplier for gathered resources - ore, logs, hides, etc. (1-100). Greater than 1 enables macroing.
Percentage increase to the base price of enhancing items with extraordinary guild tools.
If disabled, players get occasional CAPTCHA to prevent unattended resource gathering.
If enabled, players cannot learn a recipe unless their skill is high enough to craft the item.
If enabled, any player can use any recipe. If disabled, only the acquiring player/account can use them.
If enabled, targeting a node to harvest will repeat automatically.
If enabled, uses the legacy inscription crafting system.
If enabled, all creatures can detect hidden characters based on their difficulty level.
Body value for standard Wyrms. 723 = newer large model, 12 = classic wyrm, 59 = dragon body.
Percent chance an enemy will fail to dispel a summoned creature (0 = disabled, 10-90 valid).
Percentage increase to all creature hit points. 0 = normal, 100 = double HP.
Percentage difficulty increase for normal dungeons. Affects attributes, skills, fame, karma, gold, and taming requirements.
Percentage difficulty increase for difficult dungeons.
Percentage difficulty increase for challenging dungeons.
Percentage difficulty increase for hard dungeons.
Percentage difficulty increase for deadly dungeons.
If enabled, random powerful creatures (daemons, balrons, dragons, wyrms, angels, etc.) spawn and roam the land.
Chance (0-100) of safari animals (elephants, giraffes, etc.) spawning in Sosaria.
Chance (0-100) of safari animals spawning in Lodoria.
Chance (0-100) of safari animals spawning on Serpent Island.
Chance (0-100) of safari animals spawning in Kuldar.
Chance (0-100) of safari animals spawning in the Savaged Empire.
If enabled, creatures in dungeons are hidden behind walls/doors unless searching for blood. Run [buildworld after changing.
If enabled, purple named adventurers will attack nearby monsters (not just criminals/murderers/low karma characters).
Minimum minutes for creature respawn.
Maximum minutes for creature respawn.
Maximum seconds a monster can paralyze a character (decimal). Affects mummies, ants, plants, spiders. Above 10 can allow chain-paralyze.
If enabled, vendors sell everything they normally stock (some rarity % still applies).
If enabled, vendors buy everything they normally stock (some rarity % still applies).
If enabled, vendors buy tailor materials (cotton, flax, wool, cloth, string).
If disabled, vendors will not buy from players and merchant crates act as normal containers.
If enabled, merchants have unlimited gold for buying. Otherwise limited by min/max merchant gold.
If enabled, merchants will spend their remaining gold on items they cannot fully afford.
Minimum gold merchants have for commerce (only if not Rich Merchants).
Maximum gold merchants have for commerce (only if not Rich Merchants). Resets each restocking period.
If enabled, merchant gold scales by facet to encourage selling in non-default facets. WARNING: Exceeds Max Merchant Gold.
Percentage decrease to prices when players sell to vendors. 0 disables.
If enabled, prices fluctuate based on item quality, stats, and material.
If enabled, players can buy merchant crates to sell crafted items from their homes.
Percentage increase to vendor item prices. 0 = normal prices.
Percentage increase to sold resource prices (ingots, wood, etc.). WARNING: Stacks with Price Increase.
If enabled, some merchants sell large volumes of resources (ingots, boards, leather, etc.) based on rarity and location.
If enabled, vendors will not buy resources you try to sell (bandages, ingots, cloth, boards, etc.).
If enabled, some vendors have a black market with special crafted items, land-specific resources.
If enabled, custom merchants appear in settlements after [buildworld. Configure items in Info/Scripts/Merchant.cs. Max 250 items.
If enabled, house co-owners have the same permissions as owners.
If enabled, characters can use lawn tools to add outdoor items (trees, fences, etc.). Disabling refunds gold and removes tools.
If enabled, characters can use remodeling tools to add walls, doors, tiles. Disabling refunds gold and removes tools.
Days before an unused boat or magic carpet decays (decimal, minimum 5.0).
Days before an unvisited home decays (decimal, minimum 30.0). Only applies if Houses Decay is enabled.
If disabled, houses never decay and Home Decay setting is ignored.
Maximum houses an account can own. -1 for unlimited.
If enabled, players can dye construction contracts so their pre-designed home is entirely in one color.
If enabled, players can use the custom house system. Otherwise only pre-built classic houses.
If enabled, players can buy basement doors leading to a shared public basement area. Usually for multiplayer.
If enabled, items in homes never decay regardless of lockdown status. Removes storage capacity limits from all houses.
Minutes between stat gains for trainable pets (decimal, 1.0-60.0).
Multiplier for damage dealt to player-controlled NPCs (decimal). 1.0 = normal.
Percent chance enemies score a critical double-damage hit against player-controlled NPCs (0-100).
If enabled, pets match their master's notoriety. Otherwise they are blue or criminal as appropriate.
If enabled, pets can harm the player who owns them. Note: Pet abilities may still hurt owners.
If enabled, pets can harm other pets belonging to the same player.
If enabled, mounts are auto-stabled in dungeons/caves and remounted on exit. Increases difficulty.
If enabled, characters dismount when entering buildings and remount on exit.
If enabled, characters dismount when entering player homes and remount on exit.
If enabled, followers attempt to keep up when you run fast.
If enabled, followers spread out instead of stacking on top of each other.
If enabled, followers guard each other in addition to guarding you.
Extra stabled pet slots beyond the base of 2 (0-20). Additional slots come from druidism, taming, veterinary, and herding skills.
Days after taming before a pet can be bonded (0-30).
If enabled, guards instantly kill criminals/murderers. If disabled, they chase and send caught characters to prison.
If enabled, guards pay attention to enemies outside their town borders.
If enabled, guards move faster to catch criminals (only works if Guards Kill Instantly is disabled).
If enabled, jailed players only lose raw currency. If disabled, loses stackable items (potions, bandages, arrows, etc.).
If enabled, town adventurer groups may have a humanoid, pet, or summoned companion (~5% chance). Adds atmosphere.
Confirms you have reviewed all game settings and set them to your preference.
